findings.detail: Two labeled inputs (TRUSTED system prompt, UNTRUSTED user input) merge into a single Context window box feeding the LLM, with output marked 'Hijacked output'. The two-inputs-into-one-window mental model reads clearly left-to-right. Direct vs Indirect split on the untrusted side is legible (both arrow into UNTRUSTED). No overlaps, no truncation, all text readable at 1400px. Color tiers sane: context window highlighted yellow as the load-bearing merge point, output green as terminal.

source_preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (every node traces to corpus 5.1/5.2/5.4; no governance/defense/exploit-engineering nodes)
source_preflight.jargon_check: pass (LLM spelled out as 'Large Language Model')

stats.layout: flowchart LR (two inputs merge into one window)
